title: International Shared Support
subtitle: International Shared Support and Partnership Framework
summary: An international support model combining University of Iowa backing, partner relationships, and country ambassadors.
synopsis: Sutton SignWriting needs an international support model that combines University of Iowa institutional backing, cross-country partner relationships, and locally grounded ambassadors to demonstrate real-world value and strengthen long-term stewardship.

slides.title: International Shared Support
slides.summary: SignWriting is used internationally and needs a support model that matches that reality.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for international shared support.
bullets: Active relationships in Brazil, Mexico, South Korea, and beyond
slides.speech: Sutton SignWriting is used internationally, with active relationships and visible impact across multiple countries. Students, teachers, researchers, and institutions depend on its tools, websites, and data resources.
slides.notes: Frame this as global infrastructure rather than a local project.

slides.title: Proposed Support Model
slides.summary: The preferred support model combines institutional backing, international partnership, and country ambassadors.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for the proposed support model.
bullets: University of Iowa grants, gifts, and donations
slides.speech: The preferred model combines grants, gifts, and donations administered through the University of Iowa, international partner relationships that demonstrate impact, country ambassadors who support local outreach, and shared institutional participation instead of dependence on a single maintainer.
slides.notes: This is the model slide. Keep it simple and memorable.

slides.title: Why International Participation Matters
slides.summary: International relationships demonstrate educational reliance, research value, and readiness for collaboration.
slides.speech: International relationships matter for more than outreach. They show real-world educational reliance, cross-country research value, accessibility relevance, and readiness for multi-institution collaboration.
slides.notes: Brazil is the strongest example, but not the only one.

slides.title: How Partners Can Participate
slides.summary: Partners can participate through support letters, introductions, collaboration, and country-level help.
bullets: Letters of support and statements of use
slides.speech: Partner institutions do not all need to contribute in the same way. Participation may include letters of support, statements of educational or research use, introductions to institutional contacts, collaboration on proposals, and help identifying volunteer country ambassadors.
slides.notes: Make participation feel flexible and approachable.

slides.title: University of Iowa Role and Next Steps
slides.summary: The University of Iowa provides the strongest current institutional home for this work.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for University of Iowa role.
slides.speech: The University of Iowa provides the strongest current home because it can support grant framing, gifts and donations, research integration, PI alignment, and long-term stewardship. Near-term goals include identifying institutional contacts, gathering letters of support, and mapping country-level outreach priorities.
slides.notes: End with next steps, not just abstract framing.
